  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Mortar joints not tooled concave",
    "Increased risk of water entry and moisture penetration through untooled joints"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Rake out untooled mortar joints to a depth of 1/4 to 1/2 inch while mortar is still workable, or prepare for tuckpointing",
    "Re-point and tool joints to a proper concave profile to ensure water shedding"
  ],
  "rationale": "TMS 602 requires mortar joints to be properly tooled to a concave or V-profile to compact the mortar at the surface and seal the joint against water penetration. Leaving joints untooled or improperly finished on an exterior commercial wall is a non-compliant installation that will lead to water intrusion.",
  "references": [
    "TMS 602 Specification for Masonry Structures",
    "TMS 402 Building Code Requirements for Masonry Structures"
  ]
}
